meaning: [单复同]n.新干线(日本的高速客运列车)
examples: The Kingfisher and the Shinkansen Train
The Shinkansen network, operated by Japan Railways, now connects most major Japanese cities via almost 1, 550 miles of track.
The shinkansen was not experimental: it was a carefully thought out scheme based on established practice.
Uniformed hotel staff greet guests arriving by Shinkansen train trackside for an escort up to the sumptuous, chandelier-encrusted perch.
In the Japanese way, both resigned and neither was invited to the opening ceremony for the shinkansen.
